message:
feat(runtime): child-region unit test matrix and checkpoint waiver fix

tests: FuchsiaHare's comprehensive child-region unit test matrix (bd-9qli)
adding coverage for closed-parent rejection, multi-child tracking, arena
exhaustion, grandchild drain ordering, and positive Q2 verification.
50/50 quiescence tests passing.

lint: Add ASX_CHECKPOINT_WAIVER to bounded child-array scan loop in
quiescence.c parent-unlink function. Wire nested_regions.sh into e2e
manifest.

All gates green: format-check, lint, lint-checkpoint (128 waived, 0
violations), build, test.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

-{"id":"bd-20n9","title":"Child regions: E2E nested region lifecycle scenario","description":"Create an e2e test program exercising the full nested region lifecycle with detailed logging.

## Scenario
1. Open root region R0
2. Spawn tasks in R0
3. Open child regions R0.1, R0.2 under R0
4. Spawn tasks in R0.1 and R0.2
5. Open grandchild R0.1.1 under R0.1
6. Run scheduler to complete tasks in R0.1.1
7. Close R0.1.1 → verify R0.1.child_count decremented
8. Close R0.1 → wait for tasks, verify R0.child_count decremented
9. Close R0.2
10. Close R0 → full drain, verify quiescence Q1-Q4 all pass
11. Print detailed log at each step: region IDs, states, child_counts, quiescence report

## Integration
Add to e2e test suite manifest. Create tests/e2e/e2e_nested_regions.c and wire into tests/e2e/nested_regions.sh.

## Files
- tests/e2e/e2e_nested_regions.c (new)
- tests/e2e/nested_regions.sh (new)","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-03T13:59:44.579571723Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-03T14:01:48.230167776Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-20n9","depends_on_id":"bd-9qli","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T14:01:48.229475004Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id": "bd-296", "title": "EPIC: Spec Foundation and Plan-Space Canonicalization", "description": "Background:
This project is explicitly spec-first. The C implementation must be derived from extracted semantics, not line-by-line translation from Rust source.

Goal:
Produce a self-contained canonical spec suite so implementation can proceed without consulting legacy Rust internals.

Must cover (non-negotiable):
- COMPLETE lifecycle semantics for region/task/obligation/cancellation
- outcome lattice and budget algebra
- forbidden-behavior catalog (must-fail matrix)
- machine-readable invariant schema
- scenario DSL and fixture contracts
- guarantee-substitution mapping (Rust guarantees -> C mechanisms)
- explicit exclusions/deferred surfaces and open decisions

Why this serves users:
- prevents semantic drift and accidental feature amputation
- enables predictable implementation and review
- allows independent auditors/future maintainers to understand intent without archaeology.

Cross-cutting quality contract:
- Child tasks must define and link unit tests, e2e script coverage (when behavioral), and structured log/manifest artifacts or explicit rationale for non-applicability.
- Completion evidence must remain compatible with bd-66l.9 enforcement and traceability index expectations.", "status": "closed", "priority": 0, "issue_type": "epic", "created_at": "2026-02-27T00:47:41.899905539Z", "created_by": "ubuntu", "updated_at": "2026-02-27T19:36:08.134397338Z", "closed_at": "2026-02-27T19:36:08.134377281Z", "close_reason": "done", "source_repo": ".", "compaction_level": 0, "original_size": 0, "labels": ["phase-0", "phase-1", "planning", "spec"]}
@@ -185,7 +185,7 @@
 {"id": "bd-3vt.7", "title": "Evaluate optional alpha tracks (seqlock metadata and EBR/hazard reclamation) with fallback parity checks", "description": "Background:
Plan includes optional advanced tracks for parallel profile performance; adoption requires proof and rollback.

Subtasks:
- Prototype seqlock metadata path and evaluate correctness/performance tradeoffs.
- Prototype EBR or hazard-based reclamation path for ownership-sensitive structures.
- Validate fallback paths and parity behavior against baseline locking approach.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Each track has explicit go/no-go decision with evidence.
- Fallback remains implemented and verified.
- No semantic drift is introduced by adoption.", "status": "closed", "priority": 3, "issue_type": "task", "created_at": "2026-02-27T00:53:10.323728605Z", "created_by": "ubuntu", "updated_at": "2026-03-01T20:44:37.416342272Z", "closed_at": "2026-03-01T20:44:37.416319800Z", "close_reason": "done", "source_repo": ".", "compaction_level": 0, "original_size": 0, "labels": ["alpha", "parallel", "research"], "dependencies": [{"issue_id": "bd-3vt.7", "depends_on_id": "bd-2cw.7", "type": "blocks", "created_at": "2026-03-02T04:37:11Z", "created_by": "import", "metadata": "{}", "thread_id": ""}, {"issue_id": "bd-3vt.7", "depends_on_id": "bd-3vt", "type": "parent-child", "created_at": "2026-03-02T04:37:11Z", "created_by": "import", "metadata": "{}", "thread_id": ""}, {"issue_id": "bd-3vt.7", "depends_on_id": "bd-3vt.4", "type": "blocks", "created_at": "2026-03-02T04:37:11Z", "created_by": "import", "metadata": "{}", "thread_id": ""}]}
 {"id": "bd-3vt.8", "title": "Prototype time-virtualization layer for jitter and clock-anomaly replay", "description": "Background:
Idea round 3 highlights time virtualization as a lever for reproducing jitter and clock anomalies deterministically.

Subtasks:
- Define virtual-time injection API boundaries and scheduling interactions.
- Extend scenario DSL with clock anomaly events (jitter, stall, jump).
- Build replay tests showing deterministic reproduction of anomaly scenarios.
- Evaluate operational overhead and integration risks.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Provides clear go/no-go recommendation with benchmarked overhead.
- Anomaly scenarios reproduce deterministically across repeated runs.
- Fallback to physical-time mode remains intact and documented.", "status": "closed", "priority": 2, "issue_type": "task", "created_at": "2026-02-27T00:57:19.503981512Z", "created_by": "ubuntu", "updated_at": "2026-03-01T07:09:49.925389641Z", "closed_at": "2026-03-01T07:09:49.925365135Z", "close_reason": "done. Evidence: tests/unit/runtime/test_replay.c. Artifact evidence: replay digest artifacts (tools/ci/artifacts/conformance/)", "source_repo": ".", "compaction_level": 0, "original_size": 0, "labels": ["advanced", "determinism", "replay"], "dependencies": [{"issue_id": "bd-3vt.8", "depends_on_id": "bd-2n0.4", "type": "blocks", "created_at": "2026-03-02T04:37:11Z", "created_by": "import", "metadata": "{}", "thread_id": ""}, {"issue_id": "bd-3vt.8", "depends_on_id": "bd-3vt", "type": "parent-child", "created_at": "2026-03-02T04:37:11Z", "created_by": "import", "metadata": "{}", "thread_id": ""}, {"issue_id": "bd-3vt.8", "depends_on_id": "bd-hwb.13", "type": "blocks", "created_at": "2026-03-02T04:37:11Z", "created_by": "import", "metadata": "{}", "thread_id": ""}]}
 {"id": "bd-3vt.9", "title": "Define hot-reload-safe config boundaries and semantic freeze points", "description": "Background:
Idea round 3 proposes hot-reload-safe config boundaries so runtime tuning does not mutate semantics mid-flight.

Subtasks:
- Classify config fields as hot-reload-safe, freeze-at-start, or restart-required.
- Define semantic freeze points where updates are applied atomically.
- Add tests for mid-run update safety and rollback on invalid updates.
- Document operator workflow for safe live tuning.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Config reload behavior is deterministic and policy-driven.
- Unsafe mid-run updates are rejected with explicit diagnostics.
- Replay and semantic digest guarantees remain intact.", "status": "closed", "priority": 3, "issue_type": "task", "created_at": "2026-02-27T00:57:19.586821141Z", "created_by": "ubuntu", "updated_at": "2026-03-01T20:14:59.210085015Z", "closed_at": "2026-03-01T20:14:59.210057855Z", "close_reason": "done. Evidence: docs/SEMANTIC_GAP_RISK_RANKING.md, tests/unit/core/test_config_types.c. E2e/invariant evidence: tests/e2e/e2e_core_lifecycle.c", "source_repo": ".", "compaction_level": 0, "original_size": 0, "labels": ["advanced"]}
-{"id":"bd-43ny","title":"Child regions: CBMC harness for parent-child transition safety","description":"Create a CBMC-compatible harness proving parent-child region transition invariants.

## Properties to verify
1. No orphaning: if a child is allocated, parent.child_count > 0
2. No double-decrement: closing a child twice doesn't underflow parent.child_count
3. Bounded child count: child_count never exceeds ASX_MAX_REGION_CHILDREN
4. Parent-child consistency: every child's parent_id matches its parent

## Implementation
Standalone exhaustive test (same pattern as existing CBMC harnesses). Enumerate: open parent, open 1-N children, close children in various orders, verify invariants at each step.

## Files
- tests/formal/cbmc/child_region_harness.c (new)
- Makefile (add to FORMAL_CBMC_BINS with library linking)","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-03T13:59:44.448301827Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-03T14:01:48.072466444Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-43ny","depends_on_id":"bd-9qli","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T14:01:48.071728918Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"bd-9bon","title":"Docs: Standardize walking skeleton annotations in runtime files","description":"Update all walking skeleton comments in runtime source files to use a standardized format:

## Format
/* Walking skeleton (Wave A): <what it does> | Graduation: Wave <X>, <criteria> */

## Files to update (5)
1. src/runtime/blocking.c line 4: "Walking skeleton: executes blocking work inline"
   → "Walking skeleton (Wave A): executes blocking work inline synchronously | Graduation: Wave C, POSIX/Win32 pthread/thread pool adapter"

2. src/runtime/io_driver.c line 4: "Walking skeleton: ghost reactor only, no real IO"
   → "Walking skeleton (Wave A): ghost reactor only, no real IO | Graduation: Wave C, platform reactor adapters (epoll/kqueue/IOCP)"

3. src/runtime/waker.c line 4: "Walking skeleton: single-threaded, flag-based signaling"
   → "Walking skeleton (Wave A): single-threaded flag-based signaling | Graduation: Wave C, futex/condvar-based cross-thread waking"

4. src/runtime/deadline_monitor.c line 4: "Walking skeleton: single-threaded, check-on-poll model"
   → "Walking skeleton (Wave A): single-threaded check-on-poll model | Graduation: Wave C, timer-wheel-driven deadline interrupts"

5. src/runtime/parallel.c line 5: "Walking skeleton: single-threaded simulation"
   → "Walking skeleton (Wave A): single-threaded lane simulation | Graduation: Wave C, real worker threads per lane"

## Additional
Also update src/runtime/quiescence.c line 149:
   "Q2: all child regions closed (walking skeleton: always true)"
   → reference the child region epic bead ID for tracking

## Files
- src/runtime/blocking.c, io_driver.c, waker.c, deadline_monitor.c, parallel.c, quiescence.c (modify: comments only)","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-03T14:01:10.646958418Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-03T14:03:20.124221754Z","closed_at":"2026-04-03T14:03:20.124030512Z","close_reason":"Completed by bd-yrn0 standardized runtime deferred-stub annotations","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-9bon","depends_on_id":"bd-xxv4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T14:02:31.834712688Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

-{"id":"bd-9qli","title":"Child regions: Comprehensive unit tests","description":"Add thorough unit tests for the parent-child region lifecycle.

## Test cases (in tests/unit/runtime/test_quiescence.c):
1. open_child_in_open_parent: open parent, open child, verify child_count=1
2. open_multiple_children: open 3 children, verify child_count=3
3. open_child_in_closed_parent_rejected: close parent, attempt open_child → ASX_E_REGION_NOT_OPEN
4. open_child_in_poisoned_parent_rejected: poison parent → ASX_E_REGION_POISONED
5. child_close_decrements_parent: close child, verify parent.child_count decremented
6. all_children_closed_q2_passes: close all children, run quiescence check, verify q2_children_closed=1
7. open_children_q2_fails: leave one child open, verify q2_children_closed=0
8. parent_drain_blocked_by_children: close parent with open child, verify stays in CLOSING
9. parent_drain_proceeds_after_children_close: close child, then parent drain succeeds
10. max_children_exhaustion: open ASX_MAX_REGION_CHILDREN children, next → ASX_E_RESOURCE_EXHAUSTED
11. nested_grandchild: parent → child → grandchild, verify 3-level close order
12. child_of_root_vs_child_of_child: verify parent_id tracking at each level

## Logging
Each test logs: parent_id, child_count before/after, region states. Use fprintf(stderr, ...) with descriptive prefixes.

## Files
- tests/unit/runtime/test_quiescence.c (extend)","status":"in_progress","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-03T13:59:44.321682093Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-03T20:32:04.577497487Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-9qli","depends_on_id":"bd-6u8q","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T14:01:47.761228154Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-9qli","depends_on_id":"bd-cye2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T14:01:47.914581355Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
+{"id":"bd-9qli","title":"Child regions: Comprehensive unit tests","description":"Add thorough unit tests for the parent-child region lifecycle.

## Test cases (in tests/unit/runtime/test_quiescence.c):
1. open_child_in_open_parent: open parent, open child, verify child_count=1
2. open_multiple_children: open 3 children, verify child_count=3
3. open_child_in_closed_parent_rejected: close parent, attempt open_child → ASX_E_REGION_NOT_OPEN
4. open_child_in_poisoned_parent_rejected: poison parent → ASX_E_REGION_POISONED
5. child_close_decrements_parent: close child, verify parent.child_count decremented
6. all_children_closed_q2_passes: close all children, run quiescence check, verify q2_children_closed=1
7. open_children_q2_fails: leave one child open, verify q2_children_closed=0
8. parent_drain_blocked_by_children: close parent with open child, verify stays in CLOSING
9. parent_drain_proceeds_after_children_close: close child, then parent drain succeeds
10. max_children_exhaustion: open ASX_MAX_REGION_CHILDREN children, next → ASX_E_RESOURCE_EXHAUSTED
11. nested_grandchild: parent → child → grandchild, verify 3-level close order
12. child_of_root_vs_child_of_child: verify parent_id tracking at each level

## Logging
Each test logs: parent_id, child_count before/after, region states. Use fprintf(stderr, ...) with descriptive prefixes.

 {"id": "bd-aw69", "title": "EPIC: Embedded Target Matrix Gate (GATE-EMBEDDED)", "description": "## Goal
Implement GATE-EMBEDDED: cross-compilation for ARM and RISC-V targets with QEMU-based smoke
tests, validating that the C port builds and runs correctly on constrained embedded platforms.

## Why This Matters
The C port's raison d'être is portability to embedded targets that Rust's async runtime cannot
reach (bare-metal MCUs, router-class devices, automotive ECUs). Without actually building and
testing on these targets, the "portable ANSI C" claim is aspirational rather than proven.

GATE-EMBEDDED is one of the 9 non-negotiable quality gates from AGENTS.md:
"Embedded target matrix gate (cross-build + QEMU + device smoke)."

The three resource classes (R1=tight, R2=balanced, R3=roomy) defined in asx_config.h are
specifically designed for embedded deployment. The FREESTANDING and EMBEDDED_ROUTER profiles
compile with no libc dependency. This gate proves those profiles work end-to-end.

## What "Done" Looks Like
- Cross-compilation succeeds for: arm-none-eabi (Cortex-M), aarch64-linux-gnu, riscv32-unknown-elf
- QEMU user-mode runs the C test suite for aarch64 and riscv32 targets
- Bare-metal builds (arm-none-eabi) link successfully with FREESTANDING profile
- Resource class R1 builds fit within 64KB RAM budget
- make embedded-matrix passes in CI

## Architectural Context
The Makefile already has profile-parity targets that build with ASX_PROFILE_FREESTANDING and
ASX_PROFILE_EMBEDDED_ROUTER. The freestanding hooks (src/platform/freestanding/hooks.c) provide
stub implementations for platforms without libc. What's missing is the actual cross-compiler
toolchains, QEMU setup, and the CI integration to exercise them.

## Risk
- Cross-compiler toolchain installation varies by host OS
- QEMU user-mode may not perfectly emulate all target quirks
- Bare-metal linking requires a linker script and startup code
- R1 memory budget (64KB) may require tuning ASX_MAX_* constants", "notes": "## CRITICAL: EXISTING INFRASTRUCTURE AUDIT (2026-03-12)

The following ALREADY EXISTS and must NOT be recreated or modified:
- tools/ci/run_embedded_matrix.sh: Linux-musl cross-build matrix (armv7, aarch64, mipsel)
- tools/ci/run_qemu_smoke.sh: QEMU testing for Linux-musl targets
- make ci-embedded-matrix: Makefile target for Linux-musl embedded builds
- tools/ci/generate_layout_budget_report.sh: Binary size/layout reporting
- tools/ci/compute_layout_budget_delta.sh: Cross-run regression detection

## What This Epic ACTUALLY Adds (COMPLEMENTARY, NOT REPLACEMENT)
Bare-metal cross-compilation for FREESTANDING/EMBEDDED_ROUTER profiles on:
- ARM Cortex-M (arm-none-eabi-gcc) — new toolchain
- RISC-V (riscv-elf-gcc) — new toolchain
- QEMU semihosting for bare-metal binaries — new execution mode
- R1 memory budgets for .text/.bss sections — new measurements

## Subtask Inventory (8 total)
.1 Install bare-metal ARM toolchain (60m) — Linux-musl ARM already exists
.2 Install RISC-V toolchain (60m) — genuinely new
.3 QEMU semihosting setup (90m) — Linux user-mode QEMU already exists
.4 Bare-metal Makefile targets (120m) — Linux-musl targets already exist
.5 Bare-metal link validation (120m) — genuinely new
.6 R1 memory budgets (90m) — layout reporting exists for musl
.7 CI gate for bare-metal (120m) — embedded CI exists for musl
.8 QEMU e2e tests for bare-metal (180m) — QEMU smoke exists for musl", "status": "closed", "priority": 2, "issue_type": "epic", "estimated_minutes": 840, "created_at": "2026-03-12T17:06:25.215072286Z", "created_by": "ubuntu", "updated_at": "2026-03-13T01:39:35.547567356Z", "closed_at": "2026-03-13T01:39:35.546118414Z", "close_reason": "done", "source_repo": ".", "compaction_level": 0, "original_size": 0, "labels": ["embedded", "phase3", "portability"]}
@@ -273,7 +273,7 @@
 {"id": "bd-ekb3.2.2", "title": "Fix stale parallel.h header: remove future-tense 'will be added' promise", "description": "## What

Update the parallel.h header comment from "Real multi-threaded dispatch will be added
when platform threading hooks are implemented" to a factual present-tense description.

## Where

- Header: include/asx/runtime/parallel.h lines 8-10

## Before (Stale)

```c
 * Walking skeleton: single-threaded simulation of lane scheduling.
 * Real multi-threaded dispatch will be added when platform threading
 * hooks are implemented.
```

## After (Factual)

```c
 * Currently provides single-threaded lane scheduling simulation.
 * Multi-threaded dispatch requires platform threading hooks.
```

## Why

The original comment made a promise about future work ("will be added when...").
This is problematic because:
1. It implies someone is actively working on threading (they may not be)
2. It doesn't say WHAT the current code actually does (walking skeleton simulation)
3. Future-tense promises in code comments become stale the moment the developer moves on

The fix changes to present-tense factual description: here's what it does now,
here's what's needed to go further. No promises about when or if.

## General Principle

Comments should describe WHAT IS, not WHAT WILL BE. If something is planned,
track it in beads (the issue tracker), not in code comments. Comments that say
"will" or "TODO" are essentially invisible issue tickets with no assignee,
no priority, and no dependency tracking.

 {"id": "bd-ekb3.3", "title": "Verify all constant extractions and comment fixes compile and pass tests", "description": "## What

End-to-end verification that all changes from the hardcoded constants and stale comments
audit compile cleanly and pass the full test suite.

## Verification Performed

1. `make clean && make` — Clean build with -Werror, all strict warnings enabled ✓
2. Unit test suites (all passing):
   - test_cancellation: 23/23
   - test_stream: 52/52
   - test_pool: 12/12
   - test_combinator2: 44/44
   - test_io_adapter: 13/13
   - test_det_hash: 15/15
3. Behavioral equivalence: default values match original literals exactly
4. No new compiler warnings introduced

## Regression Risk Assessment

- **Zero risk**: All changes are pure refactoring (literal → named macro)
- **Default values preserved**: #ifndef guards ensure existing behavior when macros not overridden
- **No semantic changes**: Code paths are identical; only the source of the constant value changed
- **Two-site consistency**: automotive_instrument.c had the literal in two places; both now
  use the same macro, which actually IMPROVES correctness (reset and init guaranteed same value)

 {"id": "bd-ekb3.4", "title": "Audit record: constants and comments verified as correct (no change needed)", "description": "## Purpose

This bead documents items that were examined during the audit and explicitly determined
to NOT need changes. This prevents future auditors from re-investigating the same items.

## Hardcoded Values Verified as Intentionally Correct

### 1. hooks.c:62 — default_wall_clock returns 0
- **Value**: `return 0;`
- **Why correct**: This is an intentional platform hook stub. The walking skeleton runtime
  provides default hooks that are replaced at runtime init. The wall_clock hook returns 0
  because deterministic mode doesn't need real time, and non-deterministic deployments
  MUST register a real clock hook via asx_runtime_hooks_init(). Returning 0 is fail-safe:
  it makes time-dependent logic behave deterministically rather than using garbage values.

### 2. lab.c:57,93 — tick_ns defaults to 1000000 (1ms)
- **Value**: `cfg->tick_ns = 1000000ULL;`
- **Why correct**: This IS a configurable default — it's set in the asx_lab_config struct
  which users fill before calling asx_lab_init(). The literal appears in config_init (setting
  the default) and in lab_init (fallback if user left it 0). Users override it by setting
  cfg.tick_ns before init. No #define needed because the config struct IS the override mechanism.

### 3. console.c:84 — "would check isatty()"
- **Value**: Comment explaining platform-specific behavior
- **Why correct**: The comment says "A real platform adapter would check isatty() and TERM
  environment variable." This is a design note explaining what the platform hook should do,
  not a promise of future work. The CORE profile doesn't have terminal awareness by design.

### 4. plan.c:413 — "this would set up a deadline"
- **Value**: Comment explaining async vs deterministic behavior
- **Why correct**: The comment says "In a real async runtime, this would set up a deadline."
  This explains that the deterministic walking skeleton doesn't use real deadlines, which
  is the intended behavior for CORE profile. Not stale — it's architecture documentation.

### 5. Profile overload thresholds in overload_catalog.c (90%, 85%, 80%, 75%)
- **Values**: Percentages like 90, 85, 80, 75
- **Why correct**: These are intentional per-profile policy constants, not magic numbers.
  Each profile defines specific overload thresholds as part of its operational envelope.
  The README explicitly says "Profiles control operational envelopes (limits, defaults,
  instrumentation), never semantic behavior." These ARE the per-profile defaults.

### 6. Seqlock EBR spike retry limits (100, 1000)
- **Values**: `max_retries = 100` and `max_spins = 1000`
- **Why correct**: The file is `seqlock_ebr_spike.c` — a research spike for experimental
  lock-free data structures. Spike code uses hardcoded values by design; if the spike
  graduates to production, the values should be extracted at that time. Premature
  abstraction of spike code adds overhead without benefit.

### 7. Config default timeouts in config_types.c (10s, 30s, 5s)
- **Values**: Various timeout defaults
- **Why correct**: These appear in config builder functions that produce default
  configurations. Users override them via the config struct before passing to init.
  The config struct IS the customization mechanism — no #define needed.

## Methodology

Each item was found via:
- `grep -rn 'TODO\|FIXME\|HACK\|XXX' src/` (zero results)
- `grep -rni ' will \| would ' src/*.c include/*.h`
- Manual inspection of all numeric literals in function bodies across runtime/, core/, net/

Items were classified as "needs fix" or "verified correct" based on:
1. Is the value user-facing or deployment-sensitive? → needs fix
2. Is there already a config mechanism? → verified correct
3. Is it a spike/research file? → verified correct (defer until graduation)
4. Is the "will/would" describing actual planned work or explaining semantics? → varies

## Status: COMPLETED", "status": "closed", "priority": 3, "issue_type": "docs", "created_at": "2026-03-25T03:15:52.794883818Z", "created_by": "ubuntu", "updated_at": "2026-03-25T03:15:52.794883818Z", "closed_at": "2026-03-25T03:15:52.794883818Z", "source_repo": ".", "compaction_level": 0, "original_size": 0, "labels": ["audit", "completed", "documentation"], "dependencies": [{"issue_id": "bd-ekb3.4", "depends_on_id": "bd-ekb3", "type": "parent-child", "created_at": "2026-03-25T03:15:52.794883818Z", "created_by": "ubuntu", "metadata": "{}", "thread_id": ""}]}
-{"id":"bd-eq34","title":"[Epic] Child Region Tracking and Q2 Quiescence Fix","description":"Fix the only semantic contract violation in the walking skeleton: the Q2 quiescence conjunct (child regions closed) is hardcoded to 1 at src/runtime/quiescence.c:150 without actually checking.

## Background
The quiescence contract (docs/QUIESCENCE_FINALIZATION_INVARIANTS.md) requires four conjuncts:
- Q1: all tasks complete (implemented: checks r->task_count)
- Q2: all child regions closed (HARDCODED TO 1 — THIS IS THE BUG)
- Q3: all obligations resolved (implemented: checks obligations_reserved)
- Q4: cleanup stack drained (implemented: checks cleanup_drained)

The current code at quiescence.c line 150:
    /* Q2: all child regions closed (walking skeleton: always true) */
    out->q2_children_closed = 1;

This means any parent region with open child regions will incorrectly report as quiescent. The fix requires implementing a parent-child region model.

## Architecture
asx_region_slot (src/runtime/runtime_internal.h) currently has: state, task_count, task_total, generation, alive, poisoned, cleanup, capture_arena, capture_used. We need to add: parent_id, child_count, children[N].

The existing asx_region_open() creates root regions. We add asx_region_open_child(parent, &child) for hierarchical regions.

## Scope
Wave A — Critical. This is the only false assertion in the quiescence contract.

## Deliverables
1. Extended region slot struct with parent/child tracking
2. New asx_region_open_child() public API
3. Child close tracking (decrement parent's child_count on child CLOSED)
4. Real Q2 check: (r->child_count == 0) replaces hardcoded 1
5. Parent-waits-for-children drain enforcement
6. Comprehensive unit tests (parent/child lifecycle, Q2 verification, drain blocking)
7. CBMC harness for parent-child transition safety
8. E2E scenario exercising nested region lifecycle

## Risk
Touching runtime_internal.h affects sizeof(asx_region_slot) which affects static arena sizing. Must verify ASX_MAX_REGIONS * sizeof(new_slot) stays within acceptable BSS limits. children[8] adds 8*4=32 bytes + parent_id(4) + child_count(4) = 40 bytes per slot. With ASX_MAX_REGIONS=32, that's 1.25KB additional BSS.

## Files
- src/runtime/runtime_internal.h (modify: extend asx_region_slot)
- include/asx/asx_config.h (modify: add ASX_MAX_REGION_CHILDREN)
- include/asx/runtime/runtime.h (modify: add asx_region_open_child decl)
- src/runtime/lifecycle.c (modify: child open/close tracking)
- src/runtime/quiescence.c (modify: Q2 check, drain enforcement)
- tests/unit/runtime/test_quiescence.c (modify: add child region tests)
- tests/formal/cbmc/child_region_harness.c (new: CBMC harness)
- tests/e2e/e2e_nested_regions.c (new: e2e scenario)","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-04-03T13:57:06.000460053Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-03T13:57:06.000460053Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

With ASX_MAX_REGIONS=32, that's 1.25KB additional BSS.

## Files

- tests/e2e/e2e_nested_regions.c (new: e2e scenario)","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-04-03T13:57:06.000460053Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-04T01:09:14.634809708Z","closed_at":"2026-04-04T01:09:14.634542432Z","close_reason":"Child-region tracking, quiescence Q2, tests, formal harness, and e2e scenario are now complete","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

-{"id":"bd-zibt","title":"POSIX: Implement clock hooks (clock_gettime CLOCK_MONOTONIC)","description":"Implement POSIX clock hooks in src/platform/posix/hooks.c.

## What to implement
1. posix_wall_clock(void *ctx): call clock_gettime(CLOCK_MONOTONIC, &ts), return ts.tv_sec * 1e9 + ts.tv_nsec as uint64_t nanoseconds. The hook signature is: asx_time (*asx_clock_now_ns_fn)(void *ctx).
2. posix_logical_clock(void *ctx): for deterministic mode, delegate to the default logical clock (counter-based). In live mode this is unused; wall clock drives time.

## Context
The default_wall_clock() in src/runtime/hooks.c returns 0 (stub). POSIX adapter replaces this with real monotonic time. The asx_runtime_hooks.clock.now_ns_fn field is what gets populated.

## Testing
- Verify two consecutive calls return non-decreasing values (monotonicity)
- Verify returned value is plausible (within expected nanosecond range)
- Guard test with #ifdef ASX_PROFILE_POSIX

## Files
- src/platform/posix/hooks.c (implement)","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-03T13:58:32.264338908Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-03T13:58:32.264338908Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"bd-zt9e","title":"RaptorQ: Comprehensive erasure recovery unit tests","description":"Add thorough unit tests for erasure decoding in tests/unit/raptorq/test_raptorq.c.

## Test cases
1. all_symbols_present: encode+decode with all present → output matches input exactly
2. single_source_loss: drop 1 source symbol, verify recovery succeeds
3. multiple_source_losses: drop up to repair_count source symbols, verify recovery
4. max_recoverable_loss: drop exactly repair_count sources, verify recovery (boundary)
5. unrecoverable_loss: drop repair_count+1 sources → ASX_E_RESOURCE_EXHAUSTED
6. repair_symbol_loss: drop repair symbols only (all sources present) → succeeds trivially
7. mixed_loss: drop some source + some repair symbols, verify recovery when possible
8. empty_input: 0 symbols → appropriate error
9. single_symbol: 1 source, 1 repair, drop source → recover from repair
10. round_trip_determinism: same input+erasure pattern always produces same output

## Logging
Each test prints: input size, symbol count, erasure pattern, recovery status, byte-level comparison result. Use ASSERT_TRUE with descriptive messages.

## Parameterization
Test with symbol_size=64 and symbol_size=1024 to cover both small and large symbols. Test with overhead_pct=20 and overhead_pct=50.

## Files
- tests/unit/raptorq/test_raptorq.c (extend)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-03T14:00:23.945795990Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-03T20:07:07.107948886Z","closed_at":"2026-04-03T20:07:07.107683424Z","close_reason":"Completed: expanded the RaptorQ unit suite into a broader erasure matrix covering all-present parameterized cases, repair-only loss, mixed recoverable loss, unrecoverable loss beyond repair capacity, single-symbol recovery, and repeated-run determinism; validated with rch focused RaptorQ build/run, format-check, prior build/lint/test/conformance, and UBS.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-zt9e","depends_on_id":"bd-3nny","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T14:02:25.760157586Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
